title: 2 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Cleveland, OH
metaTitle: 2 Bedroom Houses for Rent in Cleveland, OH
metaDescription: 2 bedroom houses for rent in Cleveland, OH from a local rental team — typically $750-$1,100/mo, Section 8-friendly. Book a free showing today.
primaryKeyword: 2 bedroom houses for rent cleveland ohio
secondaryKeywords: two bedroom rental homes cleveland ohio
answerBox: 2 bedroom houses are the most common rental size in Cleveland, OH, typically renting for $750 to $1,100 a month. They're widely available across the city's East and Southeast side neighborhoods, and most local 2-bedroom rental homes accept Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8).

sections.h2: What does a 2 bedroom house rent for in Cleveland?
body: A 2-bedroom house in Cleveland typically rents for **$750 to $1,100 a month**, depending on condition, location, and whether utilities are included. Two-bedroom homes make up the single largest share of our rental selection — more than any other bedroom count — so this is usually the size with the most current options across the city.

For context, Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts Cleveland's citywide median 2-bedroom asking rent at **$1,100/mo**, in line with what we typically see across our own 2-bedroom homes. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally-managed apartment complexes shows a 2-bedroom **apartment** averaging **$1,818/mo** — much higher, since that figure reflects newer, amenity-heavy apartment buildings rather than the older rental houses and duplex units that make up most of Cleveland's 2-bedroom house inventory.

sections.h2: 2 bedroom rent ranges by home condition
body: Rent for a 2-bedroom home in Cleveland varies mainly by condition and size of the lot or basement, not by neighborhood prestige — Fair Housing law prohibits pricing or marketing based on who a neighborhood is 'for,' so the differences below reflect the physical home, not the area.

rows: Renovated kitchen or bath, in-unit hookups

sections.h2: Where are 2 bedroom houses available in Cleveland?
body: Two-bedroom homes are common across our East and Southeast side inventory — Slavic Village (44105), Collinwood (44110), Glenville (44108), Fairfax/Central (44104), Hough (44103), and Buckeye-Shaker (44120) — as well as a smaller number on the West side around Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102) and Old Brooklyn (44109). GCRTA's bus network reaches all of these areas, and the Blue and Green light-rail lines serve the Buckeye-Shaker corridor directly into downtown's Tower City hub.

Browse our full [houses-for-rent hub](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) for other bedroom counts and budgets, or see [3 bedroom houses for rent in Cleveland](/cleveland-rentals/houses/3-bedroom-houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio/) if you need more space.

sections.h2: What's included in a Cleveland 2 bedroom rental?
body: Most of our 2-bedroom homes are one full unit within a duplex or similar small multi-family building, though some are true single-family houses. Expect a full basement in most cases (often used for laundry hookups and storage), and a small-to-medium yard. Utility arrangements vary by property — some include water or trash, most do not include electric or gas — so always confirm exactly what's covered before signing.

Two-bedroom layouts also vary: some are one-bedroom-plus-den style with a smaller second room, while others have two comparably sized bedrooms. If you're touring a duplex unit, ask whether you'd share the basement or yard with the other unit's tenant, since that arrangement is common in Cleveland's older two- and three-family buildings and can affect day-to-day living more than the square footage alone.

Every 2-bedroom home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Voucher holders in Cuyahoga County work through the [Cuyahoga Metropolitan Housing Authority (CMHA)](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php), which sets the payment standard — the maximum monthly subsidy — for each bedroom size. CMHA's payment standards change periodically, so check the current chart directly with CMHA rather than relying on a fixed number here. See [HUD's overview of the Housing Choice Voucher program](https://www.hud.gov/helping-americans/housing-choice-vouchers) for how the program works nationally, or read our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for Cleveland-specific detail.

faq.q: How much does a 2 bedroom house rent for in Cleveland?
faq.a: Typically $750 to $1,100 a month, depending on the home's condition and features. Zumper's July 4, 2026 report puts the citywide median 2-bedroom asking rent at $1,100/mo, which matches what we generally see across our own 2-bedroom rental homes.

faq.q: Are 2 bedroom houses the most common rental size in Cleveland?
faq.a: Yes, in our available homes 2-bedroom homes outnumber every other bedroom count. They're widely available across the East and Southeast side neighborhoods we serve, including Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker.

faq.q: Is a 2 bedroom house cheaper than a 2 bedroom apartment in Cleveland?
faq.a: Often, yes. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report shows professionally-managed 2-bedroom apartments averaging $1,818/mo, well above the typical $750-$1,100 range for a 2-bedroom house, largely because that figure reflects newer apartment complexes rather than older neighborhood rental homes.

faq.q: Do 2 bedroom rental houses in Cleveland accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with, regardless of bedroom count, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. CMHA sets the payment standard for 2-bedroom vouchers in Cuyahoga County; check their current chart for the exact subsidy amount.

faq.q: What utilities come with a 2 bedroom rental in Cleveland?
faq.a: It varies by property. Some 2-bedroom homes include water or trash service, but most do not include electric or gas. Always ask which utilities are included before signing a lease, since that affects your real monthly cost.
